query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: 6d2f8951). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Answer comparison not shown when type answer into the card is enabled ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Enable `Advanced â†’ Type answer into the card` - Review a card that requires typing the answer. ### Expected behaviour After clicking "Show Answer", the comparision of the typed and actual answers should be shown. It is the case when the above-mentioned Advanced option is disabled. ### Actual behaviour Only the correct answer is shown. ### Debug info ```text 2.17 beta 4 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? This might be a long-standing bug. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/scripts/card.js` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/TypeAnswer.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Null if no answer is expected. */ 46â†’ var correct: String? = null 47â†’ private set 48â†’ 49â†’ /** What the learner actually typed (externally mutable) */ 50â†’ var input = "" 51â†’ 52â†’ /** Font face of the 'compare to' field */ 53â†’ var font = "" 54â†’ private set 55â†’ 56â†’ /** The font size of the 'compare to' field */ 57â†’ var size = 0 58â†’ private set 59â†’ 60â†’ var languageHint: LanguageHint? = null 61â†’ private set 62â†’ 63â†’ /** 64â†’ * Optional warning for when a typed answer can't be displayed 65â†’ * 66â†’ * * empty card [R.string.empty_card_warning] 67â†’ * * unknown field specified [R.string.unknown_type_field_warning] 68â†’ * */ 69â†’ var warning: String? = null 70â†’ private set 71â†’ 72â†’ /** 73â†’ * @return true If entering input via EditText 74â†’ * and if the current card has a {{type:field}} on the card template 75â†’ */ 76â†’ fun validForEditText(): Boolean { 77â†’ return !useInputTag && correct != null 78â†’ } 79â†’ 80â†’ fun autoFocusEditText(): Boolean { 81â†’ return validForEditText() && autoFocus 82â†’ } 83â†’ 84â†’ /** 85â†’ * Extract type answer/cloze text and font/size 86â†’ * @param card The next card to display 87â†’ */ 88â†’ fun updateInfo(col: Collection, card: Card, res: Resources) { 89â†’ correct = null 90â†’ val q = card.question(col) 91â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(q) 92â†’ var clozeIdx = 0 93â†’ if (!m.find()) { 94â†’ return 95â†’ } 96â†’ var fldTag = m.group(1)!! 97â†’ // if it's a cloze, extract data 98â†’ if (fldTag.startsWith("cloze:")) { 99â†’ // get field and cloze position 100â†’ clozeIdx = card.ord + 1 101â†’ fldTag = fldTag.split(":").toTypedArray()[1] 102â†’ } 103â†’ // loop through fields for a match 104â†’ val flds: JSONArray = card.model(col).getJSONArray("flds") 105â†’ for (fld in flds.jsonObjectIterable()) { 106â†’ val name = fld.getString("name") 107â†’ if (name == fldTag) { 108â†’ correct = card.note(col).getItem(name) 109â†’ if (clozeIdx != 0) { 110â†’ // narrow to cloze 111â†’ correct = contentForCloze(correct!!, clozeIdx) 112â†’ } 113â†’ font = fld.getString("font") 114â†’ size = fld.getInt("size") 115â†’ languageHint = LanguageHintService.getLanguageHintForField(fld) 116â†’ break 117â†’ } 118â†’ } 119â†’ when (correct) { 120â†’ null -> { 121â†’ warning = if (clozeIdx != 0) { 122â†’ res.getString(R.string.empty_card_warning) 123â†’ } else { 124â†’ res.getString(R.string.unknown_type_field_warning, fldTag) 125â†’ } 126â†’ } 127â†’ "" -> { 128â†’ correct = null 129â†’ } 130â†’ else -> { 131â†’ warning = null 132â†’ } 133â†’ } 134â†’ } 135â†’ 136â†’ /** 137â†’ * Format question field when it contains typeAnswer or clozes. If there was an error during type text extraction, a 138â†’ * warning is displayed 139â†’ * 140â†’ * @param buf The question text 141â†’ * @return The formatted question text 142â†’ */ 143â†’ fun filterQuestion(buf: String): String { 144â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(buf) 145â†’ if (warning != null) { 146â†’ return m.replaceFirst(warning!!) 147â†’ } 148â†’ val sb = java.lang.StringBuilder() 149â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 150â†’ if (useInputTag) { 151â†’ // These functions are defined in the JavaScript file assets/scripts/card.js. We get the text back in 152â†’ // shouldOverrideUrlLoading() in createWebView() in this file. 153â†’ append( 154â†’ """
155â†’ 0) { 160â†’ append("style=\"font-family: '").append(font).append("'; font-size: ") 161â†’ .append(size).append("px;\" ") 162â†’ } 163â†’ append(">\n
\n") 164â†’ } else { 165â†’ append("........") 167â†’ } 168â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 169â†’ } 170â†’ 171â†’ /** 172â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison: `[[type:(.+?)]]` 173â†’ * 174â†’ * Replaces with the HTML for the correct answer, and the comparison to the correct answer if appropriate. 175â†’ * 176â†’ * @param answer The card content on the back of the card 177â†’ * 178â†’ * @return The formatted answer text with `[[type:(.+?)]]` replaced with HTML 179â†’ */ 180â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String): String { 181â†’ val userAnswer = input 182â†’ val correctAnswer = correct 183â†’ Timber.d("correct answer = %s", correctAnswer) 184â†’ Timber.d("user answer = %s", userAnswer) 185â†’ return filterAnswer(answer, userAnswer, correctAnswer ?: "") 186â†’ } 187â†’ 188â†’ /** 189â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison. Show the correct answer, and the comparison if appropriate. 190â†’ * 191â†’ * @param answer The answer text 192â†’ * @param userAnswer Text typed by the user, or empty. 193â†’ * @param correctAnswer The correct answer, taken from the note. 194â†’ * @return The formatted answer text 195â†’ */ 196â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String, userAnswer: String, correctAnswer: String): String { 197â†’ val m: Matcher = PATTERN.matcher(answer) 198â†’ val sb = StringBuilder() 199â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 200â†’ 201â†’ var comparisonText = CollectionManager.compareAnswer(correctAnswer, userAnswer) 202â†’ if (doNotUseCodeFormatting) { 203â†’ comparisonText = "$comparisonText" 204â†’ } 205â†’ append(Matcher.quoteReplacement(comparisonText)) 206â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 207â†’ } 208â†’ 209â†’ companion object { 210â†’ /** Regular expression in card data for a 'type answer' after processing has occurred */ 211â†’ val PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[\\[type:(.+?)]]") 212â†’ 213â†’ fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences): TypeAnswer { 214â†’ return TypeAnswer( 215â†’ useInputTag = preferences.getBoolean("useInputTag", false), 216â†’ doNotUseCodeFormatting = preferences.getBoolean("noCodeFormatting", false), 217â†’ autoFocus = preferences.getBoolean("autoFocusTypeInAnswer", true) 218â†’ ) 219â†’ } 220â†’ 221â†’ /** 222â†’ * Return the correct answer to use for {{type::cloze::NN}} fields. 223â†’ * 224â†’ * @param txt The field text with the clozes 225â†’ * @param idx The index of the cloze to use 226â†’ * @return If the cloze strings are the same, return a single cloze string, otherwise, return 227â†’ * a string with a comma-separeted list of strings with the correct index. 228â†’ */ 229â†’ @VisibleForTesting 230â†’ fun contentForCloze(txt: String, idx: Int): String? { 231â†’ // In Android, } should be escaped 232â†’ val re = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c$idx::(.+?)\\}\\}") 233â†’ val m = re.matcher(txt) 234â†’ val matches: MutableList = ArrayList() 235â†’ var groupOne: String 236â†’ while (m.find()) { 237â†’ groupOne = m.group(1)!! 238â†’ val colonColonIndex = groupOne.indexOf("::") 239â†’ if (colonColonIndex > -1) { 240â†’ // Cut out the hint. 241â†’ groupOne = groupOne.substring(0, colonColonIndex) 242â†’ } 243â†’ matches.add(groupOne) 244â†’ } 245â†’ val uniqMatches: Set = HashSet(matches) // Allow to check whether there are distinct strings 246â†’ 247â†’ // Make it consistent with the Desktop version (see issue #8229) 248â†’ return if (uniqMatches.size == 1) { 249â†’ matches[0] 250â†’ } else { 251â†’ matches.joinToString(", ") 252â†’ } 253â†’ } 254â†’ } 255â†’} 256â†’

Null if no answer is expected. */ 46â†’ var correct: String? = null 47â†’ private set 48â†’ 49â†’ /** What the learner actually typed (externally mutable) */ 50â†’ var input = "" 51â†’ 52â†’ /** Font face of the 'compare to' field */ 53â†’ var font = "" 54â†’ private set 55â†’ 56â†’ /** The font size of the 'compare to' field */ 57â†’ var size = 0 58â†’ private set 59â†’ 60â†’ var languageHint: LanguageHint? = null 61â†’ private set 62â†’ 63â†’ /** 64â†’ * Optional warning for when a typed answer can't be displayed 65â†’ * 66â†’ * * empty card [R.string.empty_card_warning] 67â†’ * * unknown field specified [R.string.unknown_type_field_warning] 68â†’ * */ 69â†’ var warning: String? = null 70â†’ private set 71â†’ 72â†’ /** 73â†’ * @return true If entering input via EditText 74â†’ * and if the current card has a {{type:field}} on the card template 75â†’ */ 76â†’ fun validForEditText(): Boolean { 77â†’ return !useInputTag && correct != null 78â†’ } 79â†’ 80â†’ fun autoFocusEditText(): Boolean { 81â†’ return validForEditText() && autoFocus 82â†’ } 83â†’ 84â†’ /** 85â†’ * Extract type answer/cloze text and font/size 86â†’ * @param card The next card to display 87â†’ */ 88â†’ fun updateInfo(col: Collection, card: Card, res: Resources) { 89â†’ correct = null 90â†’ val q = card.question(col) 91â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(q) 92â†’ var clozeIdx = 0 93â†’ if (!m.find()) { 94â†’ return 95â†’ } 96â†’ var fldTag = m.group(1)!! 97â†’ // if it's a cloze, extract data 98â†’ if (fldTag.startsWith("cloze:")) { 99â†’ // get field and cloze position 100â†’ clozeIdx = card.ord + 1 101â†’ fldTag = fldTag.split(":").toTypedArray()[1] 102â†’ } 103â†’ // loop through fields for a match 104â†’ val flds: JSONArray = card.model(col).getJSONArray("flds") 105â†’ for (fld in flds.jsonObjectIterable()) { 106â†’ val name = fld.getString("name") 107â†’ if (name == fldTag) { 108â†’ correct = card.note(col).getItem(name) 109â†’ if (clozeIdx != 0) { 110â†’ // narrow to cloze 111â†’ correct = contentForCloze(correct!!, clozeIdx) 112â†’ } 113â†’ font = fld.getString("font") 114â†’ size = fld.getInt("size") 115â†’ languageHint = LanguageHintService.getLanguageHintForField(fld) 116â†’ break 117â†’ } 118â†’ } 119â†’ when (correct) { 120â†’ null -> { 121â†’ warning = if (clozeIdx != 0) { 122â†’ res.getString(R.string.empty_card_warning) 123â†’ } else { 124â†’ res.getString(R.string.unknown_type_field_warning, fldTag) 125â†’ } 126â†’ } 127â†’ "" -> { 128â†’ correct = null 129â†’ } 130â†’ else -> { 131â†’ warning = null 132â†’ } 133â†’ } 134â†’ } 135â†’ 136â†’ /** 137â†’ * Format question field when it contains typeAnswer or clozes. If there was an error during type text extraction, a 138â†’ * warning is displayed 139â†’ * 140â†’ * @param buf The question text 141â†’ * @return The formatted question text 142â†’ */ 143â†’ fun filterQuestion(buf: String): String { 144â†’ val m = PATTERN.matcher(buf) 145â†’ if (warning != null) { 146â†’ return m.replaceFirst(warning!!) 147â†’ } 148â†’ val sb = java.lang.StringBuilder() 149â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 150â†’ if (useInputTag) { 151â†’ // These functions are defined in the JavaScript file assets/scripts/card.js. We get the text back in 152â†’ // shouldOverrideUrlLoading() in createWebView() in this file. 153â†’ append( 154â†’ """
155â†’ 0) { 160â†’ append("style=\"font-family: '").append(font).append("'; font-size: ") 161â†’ .append(size).append("px;\" ") 162â†’ } 163â†’ append(">\n
\n") 164â†’ } else { 165â†’ append("........") 167â†’ } 168â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 169â†’ } 170â†’ 171â†’ /** 172â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison: `[[type:(.+?)]]` 173â†’ * 174â†’ * Replaces with the HTML for the correct answer, and the comparison to the correct answer if appropriate. 175â†’ * 176â†’ * @param answer The card content on the back of the card 177â†’ * 178â†’ * @return The formatted answer text with `[[type:(.+?)]]` replaced with HTML 179â†’ */ 180â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String): String { 181â†’ val userAnswer = input 182â†’ val correctAnswer = correct 183â†’ Timber.d("correct answer = %s", correctAnswer) 184â†’ Timber.d("user answer = %s", userAnswer) 185â†’ return filterAnswer(answer, userAnswer, correctAnswer ?: "") 186â†’ } 187â†’ 188â†’ /** 189â†’ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison. Show the correct answer, and the comparison if appropriate. 190â†’ * 191â†’ * @param answer The answer text 192â†’ * @param userAnswer Text typed by the user, or empty. 193â†’ * @param correctAnswer The correct answer, taken from the note. 194â†’ * @return The formatted answer text 195â†’ */ 196â†’ fun filterAnswer(answer: String, userAnswer: String, correctAnswer: String): String { 197â†’ val m: Matcher = PATTERN.matcher(answer) 198â†’ val sb = StringBuilder() 199â†’ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 200â†’ 201â†’ var comparisonText = CollectionManager.compareAnswer(correctAnswer, userAnswer) 202â†’ if (doNotUseCodeFormatting) { 203â†’ comparisonText = "$comparisonText" 204â†’ } 205â†’ append(Matcher.quoteReplacement(comparisonText)) 206â†’ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 207â†’ } 208â†’ 209â†’ companion object { 210â†’ /** Regular expression in card data for a 'type answer' after processing has occurred */ 211â†’ val PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[\\[type:(.+?)]]") 212â†’ 213â†’ fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences): TypeAnswer { 214â†’ return TypeAnswer( 215â†’ useInputTag = preferences.getBoolean("useInputTag", false), 216â†’ doNotUseCodeFormatting = preferences.getBoolean("noCodeFormatting", false), 217â†’ autoFocus = preferences.getBoolean("autoFocusTypeInAnswer", true) 218â†’ ) 219â†’ } 220â†’ 221â†’ /** 222â†’ * Return the correct answer to use for {{type::cloze::NN}} fields. 223â†’ * 224â†’ * @param txt The field text with the clozes 225â†’ * @param idx The index of the cloze to use 226â†’ * @return If the cloze strings are the same, return a single cloze string, otherwise, return 227â†’ * a string with a comma-separeted list of strings with the correct index. 228â†’ */ 229â†’ @VisibleForTesting 230â†’ fun contentForCloze(txt: String, idx: Int): String? { 231â†’ // In Android, } should be escaped 232â†’ val re = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c$idx::(.+?)\\}\\}") 233â†’ val m = re.matcher(txt) 234â†’ val matches: MutableList = ArrayList() 235â†’ var groupOne: String 236â†’ while (m.find()) { 237â†’ groupOne = m.group(1)!! 238â†’ val colonColonIndex = groupOne.indexOf("::") 239â†’ if (colonColonIndex > -1) { 240â†’ // Cut out the hint. 241â†’ groupOne = groupOne.substring(0, colonColonIndex) 242â†’ } 243â†’ matches.add(groupOne) 244â†’ } 245â†’ val uniqMatches: Set = HashSet(matches) // Allow to check whether there are distinct strings 246â†’ 247â†’ // Make it consistent with the Desktop version (see issue #8229) 248â†’ return if (uniqMatches.size == 1) { 249â†’ matches[0] 250â†’ } else { 251â†’ matches.joinToString(", ") 252â†’ } 253â†’ } 254â†’ } 255â†’} 256â†’
